note: Explore surfaced the CR is bigger than a default flip: the herdr adapter mis-routed tab (and window) to a right split, so flipping the default to tab alone was a no-op on herdr. Scope grew to teach herdr its native tab create primitive, drop the redundant window value (tmux local name for the Tab concept), and align --at to the canonical Session/Workspace/Tab/Pane vocabulary. Spec gate: 1 frozen scenario rewritten (default pane:right->tab, ratified re-open) + 3 additive (tab-not-split per backend, no-focus-steal, allowed-set); sdd-spec-judge ALIGNED.

Impl gate caught two blockers on first build: (1) tmux tab used new-window WITHOUT -d, stealing the caller focus now that tab is the default -> added -d (background); (2) the allowed-set scenario (window rejected) had no verification -> added a CLI e2e test asserting the commander choices rejection. Both fixed; re-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true; 300/300 tests + root verify 19/19.
